segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: My guest on the show today is Alice Riches who started the Street Play Scheme where she lives in Beechwood Road.
segments.zh: 今天节目上的嘉宾是Alice Riches，她在自己居住的Beechwood Road街上发起了“街道游玩计划”(Street Play Scheme)。可能有听众还不清楚——所谓Street Play，是指本地居民把自家街道封闭几个小时，让孩子们能在街上安全玩耍。她最早是在自家所在的Beechwood Road街上做起来的，后来这个点子火了，如今全城都有街道游玩计划。那么Alice，您到底是什么时候开始搞这个计划的？

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: For those of you that don't already know – Street Play involves local residents closing off their street for a few hours so that children have a chance to play in the street safely.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: She started it in her own street, Beechwood Road, and the idea caught on, and there are now Street Play Schemes all over the city.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: So when did you actually start the scheme, Alice?

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Well, I first had the idea when my oldest child was still a toddler, so that's about six years ago now – but it took at least two years of campaigning before we were actually able to make it happen.
segments.zh: 嗯，我最初有这个想法的时候，我家老大还在蹒跚学步——差不多是六年前了。但真正把它做起来之前，我们至少奔走、游说了两年。所以这个计划正式运作到现在有三年了。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: So the scheme's been up and running for three years now.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: We'd love to be able to close our road for longer – for the whole weekend, from Saturday morning until Sunday evening, for example.
segments.zh: 我们其实很希望能封路封得更久一些——比如封上整个周末，从周六早上一直到周日晚上。目前每周只封一次。刚开始那会儿，每月才封一次。所以我们还在努力争取更多时间。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: At the moment it's just once a week.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: But when we started it was only once a month. But we're working on it.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: So what actually happens when Beechwood Road is closed?
segments.zh: 那Beechwood Road封路的时候，实际上是怎么运作的？

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: We have volunteer wardens, mostly parents but some elderly residents too, who block off our road at either end.
segments.zh: 我们有志愿管理员——主要是家长，也有一些上了年纪的居民——他们负责在道路两端把路封起来。市议会给我们提供了专门的路标，但每一头总会有一位志愿者，负责向路过的司机解释情况。整体来说，司机们都能理解，我们只有一两次不得不叫警察介入。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: The council have provided special signs but there's always a volunteer there to explain what's happening to any motorists.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Generally, they're fine about it – we've only had to get the police involved once or twice.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Now I should explain that the road isn't completely closed to cars.
segments.zh: 我得先说明一下，这条路其实并没有完全禁行。只不过只有居民自家的车能开进来。如果有人真的必须进出Beechwood Road，也没问题——前提是他们的车速控制在时速20公里以下。但大多数人干脆就在这段时间不开车了，或者把车停到别的街上去。管理员在那儿只是为了拦下过路的车流。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: But only residents' cars are allowed.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: If people really need to get in or out of Beechwood Road, it's not a problem – as long as they drive at under 20 kilometres per hour.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: But most people just decide not to use their cars during this time, or they park in another street.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: The wardens are only there to stop through traffic.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: So can anyone apply to get involved in Street Play?
segments.zh: 那任何人都可以申请参加街道游玩计划吗？

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Absolutely – we want to include all kids in the city – especially those who live on busy roads.
segments.zh: 当然可以——我们希望把全城所有的孩子都纳进来——尤其是住在车流繁忙路段的孩子。恰恰是那些地方，对我们这种计划的需求最大。相比之下，比较富裕的地方，孩子们能进公园或者有大花园可以玩，而郊区一带，通常也有更多让孩子在户外玩耍的空间，所以这种需求就没那么强烈。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: It's here that demand is greatest.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Obviously, there isn't such demand in wealthier areas where the children have access to parks or large gardens – or in the suburbs where there are usually more places for children to play outside.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: I'd recommend that anyone listening who likes the idea should just give it a go.
segments.zh: 我建议正在听节目、又觉得这个想法不错的人，不妨大胆去试一试。全城居民对我们的积极反应，其实让我们挺意外的。这种支持不只来自家长。总会有几个人抱怨，但都是极少数。整体来看，大家都非常支持，说他们很高兴看到孩子们出门跑到街上玩——哪怕这样会有点吵闹。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: We've been surprised by the positive reaction of residents all over the city.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: And that's not just parents.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: There are always a few who complain but they're a tiny minority.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: On the whole everyone is very supportive and say they're very happy to see children out on the street – even if it does get quite noisy.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: There have been so many benefits of Street Play for the kids.
segments.zh: 街道游玩计划给孩子们带来了不少好处。家长们尤其喜欢一点：孩子们能到户外呼吸新鲜空气，而不是老是盯着电脑屏幕，哪怕在外面并没在做什么特别激烈的活动。当然，孩子能跟朋友一起在户外玩、又不用家长时刻在旁看着，这也很棒。不过在我看来，最大的好处是孩子们能培养出一种自信心——他们能够独立在户外活动，不需要父母陪着。另一件真正棒的事，是孩子们能认识街上的大人，就像是有了一个很大的“大家庭”。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Parents really like the fact that the kids are getting fresh air instead of sitting staring at a computer screen, even if they're not doing anything particularly energetic.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: And of course it's great that kids can play with their friends outside without being supervised by their parents – but for me the biggest advantage is that kids develop confidence in themselves to be outside without their parents.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: The other really fantastic thing is that children get to know the adults in the street – it's like having a big extended family.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: It certainly does have a lot of benefits.
segments.zh: 确实好处很多。我想换个话题，问问您参与的另一个相关项目——就是King Street上的那个。

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: I want to move on now and ask you about a related project in King Street.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好。这实际上是一次试验，我也参与其中，当地居民决定尝试减少King Street上的交通流量——那是我们这一带最繁忙的主干道——办法就是劝说人们某一天别开车。我们本来考虑过提高停车费——但觉得那样太不受欢迎——所以后来干脆改成禁止在King Street上停车，但保留其他停车场照常开放。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Well this was an experiment I was involved in where local residents decided to try and reduce the traffic along King Street, which is the busiest main road in our area, by persuading people not to use their cars for one day.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: We thought about making people pay more for parking – but we decided that would be really unpopular – so instead we just stopped people from parking on King Street but left the other car parks open.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: It was surprising how much of a difference all this made.
segments.zh: 结果这一切带来的变化超乎预料。跟我们预期的一样，空气质量明显改善了；但让我完全没想到的是，那里变得特别安静——即便公交车照常在跑。当然大家都表示感觉更安全了，但真正让我们吃惊的是，那一天沿街商店的销售额显著上升——我们原以为逛街的人会更少，没想到反而更多了。

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: As we'd predicted, air quality was significantly better but what I hadn't expected was how much quieter it would be – even with the buses still running.

segments.speaker: ALICE
segments.en: Of course everyone said they felt safer but we were actually amazed that sales in the shops went up considerably that day – we thought there'd be fewer people out shopping – not more.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: That's really interesting so the fact that …

questions.title: Questions 11-16
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.prompt: When did the Street Play Scheme first take place?

pairs.p: up and running for three years now
pairs.note: 题干问“最初开始（正式做起来）”，原文说计划“已运作三年”，即三年前正式开始。

pairs.p: three years
pairs.note: 原文与选项 B 逐字相同。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 six years ago 只是当年有了 idea，直到两年的游说之后才真正 make it happen，因此计划 up and running 已经 three years，答案 B。

items.prompt: How often is Beechwood Road closed to traffic now?

pairs.p: at the moment it's just once a week
pairs.note: 题干 now = 原文 at the moment，both 表示“当前”。

pairs.p: once a week
pairs.note: 选项 A 与原文完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 提到 whole weekend 只是希望，once a month 是过去，现在是 just once a week，答案 A。

items.prompt: Who is responsible for closing the road?

pairs.p: volunteer wardens, mostly parents but some elderly residents too, who block off our road
pairs.note: 题干“负责封路”对应原文“志愿管理员在两端封路”，wardens 即选项 C 的 wardens。

pairs.p: wardens
pairs.note: 选项 C 中的 wardens 在原文里逐字出现。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 明说 block off our road 的是 volunteer wardens，是本地志愿管理员，答案 C。

items.prompt: Residents who want to use their cars

options.text: have to park in another street.

pairs.p: need to get in or out of Beechwood Road
pairs.note: 题干“想要开车”对应原文“需要开车进出这条路”。

pairs.p: drive at under 20 kilometres per hour
pairs.note: 题干“开得很慢”对应原文“时速 20 公里以下”，是数字化的具体说法。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说进出可以，as long as they drive at under 20 kilometres per hour，即必须开得很慢，答案 B。

items.prompt: Alice says that Street Play Schemes are most needed in

pairs.p: especially those who live on busy roads
pairs.note: 题干“最需要”对应原文 especially 强调，busy roads 即车多的地方。

pairs.p: on busy roads
pairs.note: busy roads = areas with heavy traffic，同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 especially those who live on busy roads，即车流繁忙的地方最需要，答案 C。选项 A、B 分别对应 wealthier areas、suburbs，Alice 明确说这两处需求较小。

items.prompt: What has been the reaction of residents who are not parents?

options.text: Many of them were unhappy at first.
They like seeing children play in the street.
They are surprised by the lack of noise.

pairs.q: like seeing children play in the street
pairs.p: happy to see children out on the street
pairs.note: 题干 like seeing = 原文 happy to see，同义；children play in the street = children out on the street。

pairs.p: everyone is very supportive
pairs.note: 题干“居民的反应”对应原文“大家都很支持”。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 on the whole everyone is very supportive and say they're very happy to see children out on the street，even if it does get quite noisy —— 反驳选项 C，答案是选项 B。

questions.title: Questions 17 and 18
instructions: Which TWO benefits for children does Alice think are the most important?

items.prompt: Which TWO benefits for children does Alice think are the most important? (first answer)

options.text: opportunity to be part of a community

pairs.p: develop confidence in themselves to be outside without their parents
pairs.note: 题选 B 独立感对应原文“培养自信、能独立不需父母陪”，independence = without their parents。

pairs.p: the biggest advantage
pairs.note: 题干 most important = 原文 biggest advantage。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 the biggest advantage 是 kids develop confidence in themselves to be outside without their parents，等同于选项 B“独立意识增强”。

items.prompt: Which TWO benefits for children does Alice think are the most important? (second answer)

options.text: opportunity to be part of a community

pairs.p: like having a big extended family
pairs.note: 题选 D“融入社区”对应原文“像一个大家庭”，家庭/社区 都是群体归属感。

pairs.p: children get to know the adults in the street
pairs.note: 题干 opportunity to 对应原文 get to know，都是“有机会去”做某事。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 children get to know the adults in the street – it's like having a big extended family，即孩子融入更大的“社区/大家庭”，对应选项 D。

questions.title: Questions 19 and 20
instructions: Which TWO results of the King Street experiment surprised Alice?

items.prompt: Which TWO results of the King Street experiment surprised Alice? (first answer)

pairs.p: sales in the shops went up considerably that day
pairs.note: 题选 A more shoppers 对应原文“商店销售额显著上升”，两者都意味着顾客更多。

pairs.p: we were actually amazed
pairs.note: 题干 surprised = 原文 amazed，同义。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 we were actually amazed that sales in the shops went up considerably, we thought there'd be fewer people out shopping – not more —— 对应选项 A more shoppers。

items.prompt: Which TWO results of the King Street experiment surprised Alice? (second answer)

pairs.p: how much quieter it would be
pairs.note: 题选 E“噪音污染减少”对应原文“变得多么安静”，quieter = less noise pollution。

pairs.p: what I hadn't expected was
pairs.note: 题干 surprised = 原文 hadn't expected，都是“出乎意料”。

paraphrase.explain: Alice 说 what I hadn't expected was how much quieter it would be，改善空气质量是预期之内（不算 surprised），而“变安静”才是让她吃惊的，对应选项 E less noise pollution。
